messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following debt collection agency privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"DebtResolve processes your name, address, outstanding balance, payment history, and contact details to recover debts on behalf of our clients. We process this data under legitimate interest to recover lawfully owed debts. We may contact you by phone, letter, or email. Data is shared with credit reference agencies. Records are kept for 6 years from the date of last activity. You can request access to your data by writing to dataprotection@debtresolve.com. We will notify the ICO within 72 hours of a breach. Our DPO is at dpo@debtresolve.com."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PARTIAL] Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)) stated for debt recovery. A legitimate interest assessment should be documented and referenced, as debt recovery contact can be intrusive.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Financial distress and debt can be associated with health conditions or vulnerability. No safeguard for vulnerable data subjects is mentioned.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Debt recovery is a clear and specific purpose.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is standard and proportionate for debt collection.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] 6 years from last activity is stated and standard for financial debt records.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Only access right mentioned. Objection (Art. 21) is particularly relevant for legitimate interest processing and must be prominently stated. Other rights absent.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PARTIAL] Credit reference agencies referenced but not named. Client creditors not addressed.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [FAIL] Not addressed. Credit reference agencies may have non-EEA operations.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO at dpo@debtresolve.com disclosed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 72-hour ICO notification stated.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (LIA not referenced), Art. 15-21 (objection right and most other rights absent), Art. 28 (credit reference agencies not named), Art. 46 (transfer safeguards not addressed)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Reference the documented LIA for debt recovery legitimate interest
- Prominently state the right to object to processing under Art. 21
- Add access, rectification, erasure, portability, and restriction rights
- Name specific credit reference agencies used
- Clarify controller/processor relationship with client creditors
- Address international transfer safeguards for credit reference agencies
- Add vulnerability safeguard policy for financially distressed individuals
